name: TMA personal income capture MVP
description: Add a personal-only income capture slice for `apps/tma` and `apps/worker`. Worker gets a separate `incomes` table plus authenticated `GET /api/v1/incomes` and `POST /api/v1/incomes` routes. TMA gets `/incomes` and `/incomes/new`, a one-page add-income form that visually reuses expense step 2, a Home shortcut entrypoint, and separate income query keys. MVP always stores `categoryKey='money-in'`, has no household/group behavior, and does not touch expense persistence.

planned: Add a separate worker income persistence path with mirrored expense-like columns, personal-only semantics, and strict server-side `money-in` assignment.
Add TMA income list + one-page add-income routes with Telegram-native back/bottom button behavior and no reuse of the expense multi-step store.
Add focused worker/TMA verification plus product-spec, plan-index, and harness updates so repo truth matches shipped behavior.
done: Worker now ships `apps/worker/migrations/0005_incomes.sql`, `apps/worker/src/routes/incomes.ts`, income contracts/repositories/handlers, and split worker income integration specs for authenticated personal-only `GET /api/v1/incomes` and `POST /api/v1/incomes` backed by a dedicated `incomes` table.

The worker create/list contract aligns with the approved MVP: server-owned `categoryKey='money-in'`, request `sourceKey`, response `spentByUserId`, VND-only amount normalization, `source_key` filtering, stable cursor pagination, and `deleted_at IS NULL` filtering so soft-deleted rows stay hidden from the list surface.

TMA now exposes `/incomes` and `/incomes/new` through `apps/tma/src/app/router/app-router.tsx`, adds a Home shortcut entrypoint, and implements a route-local one-page add-income form with Telegram `BottomButton` save behavior, source selection, and success navigation back to the income list.

The TMA income list uses a dedicated income feature module with cursor-based infinite query wiring, separate cache keys, correct stored-minor currency rendering, and a load-more affordance so incomes beyond the worker default page limit remain reachable.

PR review follow-up polished the add-income route with local `YYYY-MM-DD` date handling, optional note capture/payload support, and single-source error haptic behavior instead of duplicate failure feedback.

A TMA navigation follow-up changed add-income success handling from `navigate(TMA_PATHS.incomes)` to `navigate(-1)` so saving returns to the existing income-list screen instead of pushing a duplicate `/incomes` entry onto the SPA history stack.

Runtime truth was documented in `docs/product-specs/tma/income-capture.md`, the TMA product-spec index now links it, and the feat-125 ExecPlan/index/harness records were updated through completion with verification evidence and the post-review fixes.
verification: `pnpm --filter worker exec vitest run test/integration/incomes-create.spec.ts test/integration/incomes-create-validation.spec.ts test/integration/incomes-list-content.spec.ts test/integration/incomes-list-deleted.spec.ts test/integration/incomes-list-errors.spec.ts` passed with 16/16 tests after the pre-push split and preserved the full worker income coverage.

`pnpm --filter tma exec vitest run src/test/incomes-api.test.ts` passed after the initial income feature implementation and again after the post-review pagination/amount-display follow-up (2/2 tests green).
`./init.sh typecheck` and `./init.sh lint` returned `OK` after the income navigation-stack follow-up changed add-income success to history back-navigation.
`./init.sh` returned `Done!` after the income navigation-stack follow-up, confirming the final repo state stayed green after the add-income history fix and harness updates.
`./init.sh build` returned `OK` after the TMA follow-up fixes.
`./init.sh` returned `Done!` after the worker soft-delete regression fix and again after the final TMA follow-up fixes.

`@oracle` review initially blocked ship on two TMA amount-display bugs and flagged pagination/soft-delete concerns; the follow-up implementation fixed the blockers, the worker list path now excludes soft-deleted rows, and the PR review follow-up fixed local-date, note, and duplicate-error-haptic issues before merge prep.

notes: This remains an intentionally narrow MVP. It does not add income analytics, summaries, edit/delete/detail flows, or any web surface. Telegram-native manual QA in a real Telegram WebView was not run in this session, so remaining risk is limited to safe-area/keyboard/BottomButton feel on-device rather than API or type/build correctness. If execution later needs persisted multi-route draft state, record that decision in the ExecPlan before adding a Zustand store.
